scope: WIN-033 only — propagate decider partial patch to two remaining stale fields
context: Decider patched WIN-033.detail_evidence on 2026-05-09 updating Polaris luminosity from ~1,260 to ~2,400 solar luminosities (per ATT-2026-05-09T13-21-29-932Z-WIN-033, modern Hipparcos+Gaia consistent with 433 ly distance and observed V-band magnitude). The patch was applied to detail_evidence only; finding and tldr_evidence still carry the stale ~1,260 L☉ value, producing intra-WIN inconsistency. This EXP propagates the decider patch to the two remaining fields. Substantive scientific argument is unchanged — the magnitude difference between Polaris and Sigma Octantis is still fully explained by intrinsic luminosity. The new value actually IMPROVES consistency with observed photometry: predicted m_Polaris − m_SigOct = −3.60 (using L_Polaris=2,400, L_SigOct=40, d_Polaris=433 ly, d_SigOct=294 ly) vs. observed ~−3.4; old value gave −2.91, less consistent. So the propagation strengthens, not weakens, the Refuted-by-Data verdict.

patches.rationale: Propagate decider patch to finding field. Detail_evidence already updated; finding was missed by partial patch.
Propagate decider patch to tldr_evidence field. Detail_evidence already updated; tldr was missed by partial patch.

patch_instructions: Apply both find/replace patches verbatim. Each substring is unique within its target field (verified count=1). Field lengths are unchanged because both replacements are 1-for-1 character-identical width ("1,260" → "2,400" is 5 chars to 5 chars).
cross_win_consistency_note: WIN-033 cites Polaris L for the intrinsic-brightness explanation. The neighbouring WINs (Sigma Octantis comparators) do not duplicate the Polaris L value, so no cross-WIN propagation is required. WIN-005 (Polaris circumpolar visibility) does not depend on luminosity. WIN-031 and WIN-032 reference Polaris position, not luminosity. Verified by grep across data/wins.json for stale 1,260 references — only WIN-033 finding and tldr_evidence carry the stale value.
kernel_of_truth: The decider correctly identified that ~1,260 L☉ was outdated. Modern bolometric analyses with Gaia parallax (433 ly distance) and standard F7Ib bolometric corrections converge on ~2,000–2,500 L☉ for Polaris Aa. The 2,400 value is well-supported. Our prior 1,260 figure traced to older Hipparcos analyses (Turner et al. 2013 era) and was less consistent with observed magnitude difference between Polaris and Sigma Octantis. The decider was right to update.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not refresh the Hipparcos URL too — the cited source page may not directly show 2,400 L☉.
anticipated_objections.response: The Hipparcos URL is a portal landing page (cosmos.esa.int/web/hipparcos), not a specific paper, and serves as a generic reference for parallax-based stellar parameters. The luminosity number is now cited generically as ~2,400 L☉ without a specific source-paper claim — the dual-survey (Hipparcos + Gaia) framing in tldr_evidence already covers the broader provenance. A more rigorous citation upgrade is possible (e.g., to Bond et al. 2018 HST-based mass/age, or to Anderson 2019 Gaia-based luminosity) but is out of scope for this propagation patch.

anticipated_objections.objection: Should we also note that recent analyses (~2018-2023) suggest even higher values, ~3,000 L☉?
anticipated_objections.response: No. The decider chose 2,400 for the propagation; we honour that choice. 2,400 is squarely in the modern literature range and any tighter precision is beyond what the WIN argument requires (the explanation works at any value > ~30× SigOct).

test_plan: tests/run.sh --full will re-classify the snapshot. The luminosity number change is text-only, no chart data or computed counts depend on it. Numerically: compute predicted m_Polaris−m_SigOct = −2.5*log10(L_Pol/L_SigOct) + 5*log10(d_Pol/d_SigOct). With L=2,400/40 ratio = 60, d=433/294 ratio = 1.473, predicted Δm = −3.60. Observed ≈ −3.4. Deviation 0.2 mag is within the F7Ib/F0III stellar bolometric correction uncertainty (~0.2-0.3 mag).
closure_recommendation_for_decider: After applying both patches, mark ATT-2026-05-09T13-21-29-932Z-WIN-033 resolved (analyst will mark it resolved at end of this run independently, but the patch landing closes the propagation loop). No new ISS needed — this is a clean propagation of a decider-applied patch to fields the decider missed.
scope_note: This is NOT a defense neutralization (despite ATT origin in 13:21Z decider-patches batch). The defense item for WIN-033 is EXP-299 (separate; addresses pole-region pivot via Canopus δ=-52° point). EXP-300 is purely a data-consistency propagation.
tldr: Decider patched WIN-033 detail_evidence updating Polaris luminosity ~1,260 → ~2,400 L☉, but missed the same number in finding and tldr_evidence. Two find/replace patches propagate the fix. Sanity-check: new value gives better predicted Polaris−SigOct magnitude difference (−3.60 vs observed −3.4) than old (−2.91), so the propagation strengthens the Refuted-by-Data argument.
